question_text: In the Northern Hemisphere, why are the days longer in summer than in winter?
correct_answer: In summer, the Northern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun, so the Sun appears higher in the sky and is visible for more hours of the day.
explanation: When the Northern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun (summer), the Sun takes a higher path across the sky and spends more time above the horizon. In winter, the tilt is away, so the Sun is lower and visible for fewer hours.
